{"analysis":"I can see fragments: `PASSWORD=8XDP5Q2RT9Z` at one location and `K7VB3BV4WW54` near offset 3145716, just before a PK central directory header. The file appears to be a zip archive. I need to properly carve and extract it. Let me find the end-of-central-directory record and reconstruct the zip file correctly.","plan":"1. Find the EOCD (PK\x05\x06) marker in the file to determine the zip boundary
2. Carve the complete zip and try to extract
3. Also directly examine the raw data to reconstruct the password","commands":[{"keystrokes":"perl -e 'local $/; open(F,"<",$ARGV[0]); $d=<F>; while($d=~/(PK\x05\x06)/g){ printf "EOCD at %d\n", pos($d)-4}' /app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat
","duration":1.0},{"keystrokes":"dd if=/app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat bs=1 skip=1048576 count=2097280 2>/dev/null > /tmp/carved2.zip; unzip -o /tmp/carved2.zip -d /tmp/extracted2 2>&1
","duration":2.0}]}
